response.value: I'll help you extract all the words from the StarDict dictionary and save them to a text file. Let me first examine the dictionary files to understand their structure.

Now let me check if there's an existing script in the project that might help with parsing:
Let me check the existing JavaScript files to see if there's already some parsing logic:
Now I need to understand the StarDict format better. The `.idx` file contains the index with word entries. Let me examine it to understand how to extract the words:
I can see that the StarDict index file contains words separated by null bytes (00). Let me create a Node.js script to parse the index file and extract all the words:

edits.text: * Extract words from a StarDict .idx file
// Read the index file as a buffer
// Null byte indicates end of word
// Skip the next 8 bytes (offset and length in the .dict file)
i += 9; // +1 for the null byte, +8 for offset/length
// Add the last word if it exists
console.error('Error reading StarDict index file:', error.message);
* Save words to a text file
* @param {Array<string>} words - Array of words to save
* @param {string} outputPath - Path to save the text file
console.error('Error saving words to file:', error.message);
// Check if the index file exists

console.log('No words found in the dictionary');
